text.title: lint-ruff-and-biome-as-default Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for lint-ruff-and-biome-as-default. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: For every Python source file, the agent MUST run `ruff check --fix` and then `ruff format` after each edit; for every JS/TS/JSX/TSX source file, the agent MUST run `biome check --write` after each edit. Repositories MUST NOT also configure black, flake8, isort, autoflake, pyupgrade, ESLint, or Prettier — these tools' configs (.flake8, .eslintrc, .prettierrc) MUST be removed when migrating.
rule.rationale: One canonical formatter per language gives the agent a fixed, sub-second autofix step; the formatter's output is the file's canonical form, so subsequent edits never trigger formatting churn. Both tools are single static Rust binaries with no plug-in resolution, which is why their cold-start beats the legacy chain by 10–100x and makes the inner loop reflexion-friendly.

rule.statement: Python projects MUST place all ruff configuration in `pyproject.toml` under the `[tool.ruff]` and `[tool.ruff.lint]` tables; the legacy files `.flake8`, `setup.cfg [flake8]`, `tox.ini [isort]`, `pyproject.toml [tool.black]`, `pyproject.toml [tool.isort]` MUST be removed during migration. JavaScript/TypeScript projects MUST place all biome configuration in `biome.json` (or `biome.jsonc`) at the project root with the `$schema` field set; `.eslintrc*`, `.prettierrc*`, and `.editorconfig` (for JS-only repos) MUST be removed.
rule.rationale: A single config file means the agent reads one place to know what rules apply. Stale `.eslintrc` next to `biome.json` confuses contributors and IDEs alike, leaks rules that biome does not honor, and breaks the determinism that the formatter's autofix loop depends on.
